surfaces.failureGuidance: warn and fail checks must include fix_hint or nextActions
support bundles must include redaction policy, diagnostic commands, and next actions before sharing
blocking release errors must include structured remediation
generated project verification failures must report command, status, bounded output, error, and rerun guidance

generatedFailureReport.ciArtifactUsage: Attach the bounded verification result object as a CI artifact and rerun only the command named in nextActions after fixing the reported error.

remediationHandoff.commitPolicy: aiflow may queue tasks, run diagnostics, and produce remediation plans, but it must not create commits or push; commits are made by the current agent or human after gates pass
remediationHandoff.completionPolicy: mark the aiflow task complete only after the current agent or human records a commit with passing gates and no runtime state staged

p9RemediationCloseout.runtimeStatePolicy: Do not stage .aiflow, .harness, .tmp-test, .trae, coverage.out, or docs/superpowers while resolving remediation tasks.
p9RemediationCloseout.completionPolicy: Only mark GOFLY-GOV-10P9-09 complete after dx-troubleshooting-check and CLI JSON contract gates pass and no runtime state is staged.
p9RemediationCloseout.nextActionPolicy: Every failed doctor, release, generated-project, or support-bundle path must expose nextActions or error.remediation before aiflow can queue bounded remediation work.

p13CliDoctorTroubleshootingLoop.objective: Close the CI and agent troubleshooting loop so doctor, release, support bundle, and generated project failure surfaces always expose concrete next actions.
runtimeEvidence: doctor checks with warn or fail status must include fix_hint or nextActions
release check strict blockers must emit error.remediation
support bundle output must include gofly.support_bundle.v1 commands, redaction, and nextActions
generated project verification failures must include bounded output and data.nextActions

rows.failureMode: warn or fail checks without actionable guidance
rows.remediation: Fix the named doctor check first, then rerun gofly doctor --json before changing generated code or release assets.

rows.failureMode: strict release blocker without structured remediation
rows.remediation: Treat the blocker as release governance work and attach the release check JSON when queueing aiflow remediation.

rows.failureMode: support bundle without redaction policy, commands, or next actions
rows.remediation: Collect only the redacted support bundle and keep commit ownership with the current agent or human.

rows.failureMode: generated project verification failure without bounded rerun guidance
rows.remediation: Attach the bounded generated failure report and rerun only the command named by data.nextActions after fixing the cause.

p13CliDoctorTroubleshootingLoop.runtimeStatePolicy: Do not stage .aiflow, .harness, .tmp-test, .trae, coverage.out, bench/current.txt, bench/regression-report.json, bench/summary.md, bin/gofly, or docs/superpowers while resolving P13 CLI troubleshooting tasks.
p13CliDoctorTroubleshootingLoop.completionPolicy: Mark GOFLY-P13-11-CLI-DOCTOR-TROUBLESHOOTING-LOOP complete only after dx-troubleshooting-check, CLI JSON golden tests, governance-report-check, and git status review prove no runtime state is staged.

supportWorkflow: attach generated project verification failure reports when available

troubleshootingAdoptionLoop.runtimeStatePolicy: Do not stage .aiflow, .harness, .tmp-test, .trae, coverage.out, or docs/superpowers while resolving troubleshooting tasks.

steps.handoffBoundary: Use doctor output to decide whether to fix local toolchain state before editing generated or runtime code.
Treat strict release blockers as release governance tasks and attach remediation before opening aiflow work.
Share only redacted support bundles and keep commit ownership outside aiflow.
Attach bounded generated verification failures and rerun only the command named by nextActions.

remediationLoopContract.purpose: Tie doctor JSON, release check JSON, support bundle redaction, generated project failure reports, migration proof, dashboard fields, and next actions into one recoverable support workflow.

sourceContracts.adopterAction: Fix local toolchain or environment warnings before editing generated code or opening aiflow remediation work.
Treat strict release blockers as release governance tasks and attach remediation evidence before continuing adoption.
Share only redacted support bundle evidence and keep commit ownership with the current agent or human.
Attach bounded generated verification output and rerun only the command named by nextActions.

migrationRoutes.rollbackAction: Keep Gin route active behind the existing router until sampled responses and metrics match.
migrationRoutes.supportBundleAction: Attach gofly bug --json only after redaction and include sampled response, OpenAPI, and error-envelope drift evidence.

migrationRoutes.rollbackAction: Keep go-zero and gofly services addressable through discovery and switch routing back to go-zero.
migrationRoutes.supportBundleAction: Attach gofly bug --json with generated-project failure reports and release check output when scaffold compatibility fails.

migrationRoutes.rollbackAction: Restore the previous Kratos deployment target while keeping shared domain services unchanged.
migrationRoutes.supportBundleAction: Attach gofly bug --json with rendered cloud-native assets, topology output, and health or discovery drift evidence.

migrationRoutes.rollbackAction: Route latency-critical methods back to Kitex and retain gofly for REST ingress or governance surfaces.
migrationRoutes.supportBundleAction: Attach gofly bug --json with RPC boundary, resolver, balancer, stream, trace, auth, and benchmark evidence before escalating.

remediationLoopContract.nextActionPolicy: Every remediation row must identify a source command, stable fields, nextActionSource, dashboard evidence, adopter action, gate, and rollback or handoff boundary before aiflow can queue work.

workflowRows.handoffAction: Fix local toolchain, environment, and generator preconditions before opening remediation work.
Treat release blockers as governance tasks and attach release evidence before promotion.
Share only redacted support bundles with commands, redaction policy, and next actions.
Compare control-plane snapshots before changing runtime, discovery, governance, or generated project behavior.
Attach bounded generated project verification output and rerun only the command named by nextActions.

p10AINativeSupportBundle.redactionPolicy: Support bundles and generated failure reports must redact Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie, token, secret, password, and provider credential values before sharing.
p10AINativeSupportBundle.runtimeStatePolicy: Do not stage .aiflow, .harness, .tmp-test, .trae, coverage.out, or docs/superpowers while resolving AI-native support tasks.
p10AINativeSupportBundle.commitPolicy: aiflow may queue remediation tasks and produce bounded failure reports, but commits remain owned by the current agent or human after gates pass.
